refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and travel speed within safety envelope; allocate cycle time budgets to load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; implement closed-loop control with feedback.
DP-2.B: Minimize teleoperation network latency via low-latency communication protocol and edge processing; implement predictive operator input buffering to mask network jitter.
DP-2.C: Design safety services to operate in parallel with primary control path (fail-safe, non-blocking) to minimize cycle time overhead; pre-compute safety constraints offline.
DP-2.D: Implement real-time telemetry pipeline (vehicle → Network Link → Telemetry interface) with sub-100ms sampling and p95 latency ≤ 500ms to enable cycle time monitoring and root-cause analysis.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Integrated cycle time optimization across vehicle actuation (DP-2.A), teleoperation latency (DP-2.B), safety constraint enforcement (DP-2.C), and telemetry-driven monitoring (DP-2.D). Cycle time is a system-level KPI that couples vehicle performance, operator control responsiveness, and safety overhead; no single DP is sufficient.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation speed (DP-2.A) is constrained by safety limits enforced by Safety Services (DP-2.C); increasing speed may trigger safety interventions that extend cycle time.
Teleoperation latency (DP-2.B) directly impacts operator reaction time and travel precision; high latency may force slower travel speeds to maintain safety, extending cycle time.
Telemetry sampling rate (DP-2.D) must be fast enough to detect cycle time anomalies but not so fast as to consume network bandwidth needed for Tasking commands.
Safety overhead (DP-2.C) and vehicle actuation speed (DP-2.A) are coupled: aggressive acceleration profiles may require more frequent safety checks, increasing overhead.
coupling_check.notes: This is a coupled design at the system level. The FR (cycle time KPI) cannot be satisfied by optimizing any single DP in isolation. Decomposition into sub-FRs and assignment to specific modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) is necessary to decouple and allocate responsibility.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within the physical constraints of the vehicle (hydraulic/electric response time, travel distance, dump mechanism cycle time).
Assumption: Network infrastructure supports low-latency teleoperation (latency_budget ≤ 200ms assumed; if actual network latency > 200ms, cycle time target may be unachievable).
Assumption: Safety constraints can be enforced with overhead < safety_overhead_budget (e.g., < 5% of total cycle time); if safety overhead is higher, CT_target may be unachievable.

Risk: Duty cycle definition is ambiguous (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions). Cycle time performance is highly sensitive to duty cycle; if actual duty cycle differs from agreed definition, measured p95 may exceed CT_target.
Risk: Operator skill and tele-operator latency tolerance vary; cycle time may be limited by operator control responsiveness rather than vehicle capability.
Risk: Safety system activation (e.g., emergency stop, stability control) introduces non-deterministic latency; p95 cycle time may be driven by rare safety events rather than nominal operation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-2 into sub-DPs aligned with sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) and assign each to a specific module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution). Establish explicit cycle time budgets and latency allocations for each sub-system. Define duty cycle and acceptance test conditions with high precision (load mass, travel distance, terrain profile, ambient conditions, operator skill level).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration module responsible for cycle time KPI achievement. Owns the cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-systems. Owns the telemetry pipeline and KPI reporting. Coordinates sub-system performance to meet p95 ≤ CT_target.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time telemetry (load start timestamp, travel start/end timestamps, dump end timestamp, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety intervention events) to Site Manager and System Engineer for KPI verification and root-cause analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives load/travel/dump task commands from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Tasking interface. Task definition includes load mass, travel destination, and expected cycle time target.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts and constraints from Safety Services (e.g., speed limits, stability warnings, emergency stop signals) that may impact cycle time performance.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to coordinate teleoperation latency, transmit telemetry, and receive safety alerts. Network Link performance (latency, jitter, bandwidth) directly impacts cycle time KPI.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration module and the natural owner of the cycle time KPI. It owns the budget allocation, telemetry pipeline, and coordination logic. Minimal diffusion: only the sub-system implementations (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are external.

Vehicle owns the actuation performance (DP-2.A) but does not own teleoperation latency (DP-2.B), safety constraint enforcement (DP-2.C), or telemetry aggregation (DP-2.D). Cycle time KPI is not achievable by Vehicle alone; high diffusion across Teleoperation and Safety Services.

Teleoperation owns latency optimization (DP-2.B) but does not own vehicle actuation (DP-2.A), safety enforcement (DP-2.C), or system-level telemetry (DP-2.D). Cycle time KPI requires coordination with Vehicle and Safety Services; high diffusion.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement (DP-2.C) but does not own vehicle actuation (DP-2.A), teleoperation latency (DP-2.B), or telemetry (DP-2.D). Cycle time KPI is not achievable by Safety Services alone; high diffusion across other modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of the cycle time KPI (FR-2) because it is the system-level integration module responsible for coordinating Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to achieve the KPI. The DP (integrated cycle time optimization) is inherently a system-level concern that cannot be localized to a single sub-system. NGHE Solution owns the telemetry pipeline, budget allocation, and coordination logic.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it owns too much coordination logic. Recommend clear separation of concerns: NGHE Solution owns KPI definition and telemetry aggregation; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective sub-DPs and report performance metrics to NGHE Solution.

Risk: Telemetry interface (Telemetry) must be designed to support real-time cycle time monitoring with sufficient granularity (sub-cycle timestamps, latency events, safety interventions). If Telemetry interface is too coarse-grained, root-cause analysis may be impossible.

Risk: Network Link performance (latency, jitter, bandwidth) is a critical dependency for both Teleoperation (DP-2.B) and Telemetry (DP-2.D). If Network Link is unreliable or high-latency, cycle time KPI may be unachievable. Recommend explicit SLA for Network Link latency and jitter.

Risk: Safety Services may impose hard constraints (e.g., maximum speed, mandatory safety checks) that make CT_target unachievable. Recommend early coordination between Safety Manager and Project Manager to ensure safety constraints are compatible with cycle time target.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit interfaces and performance contracts between NGHE Solution and sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Define cycle time budget allocation (e.g., load: 30s, travel: 60s, dump: 20s, safety overhead: 5s, total: 115s). Require each sub-module to report performance metrics (actual sub-cycle duration, latency, safety intervention events) to NGHE Solution via Telemetry interface. Implement a cycle time monitoring dashboard in NGHE Solution that aggregates telemetry and alerts Site Manager if p95 cycle time approaches CT_target.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (integrated cycle time optimization across four sub-DPs) is coupled and does not satisfy the independence axiom of Axiomatic Design. The DP cannot be decomposed into independent sub-DPs without further refinement. Recommendation: Decompose FR-2 into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) and assign each to a specific module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution) before proceeding to IM assignment. This will enable independent DP selection for each sub-FR.

diffusion_risks: The DP (integrated cycle time optimization) is diffused across four modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution). No single module can be assigned full responsibility for the DP; coordination and integration logic must be distributed.

Telemetry interface must be implemented by all four modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution) to report cycle time metrics. This creates a diffusion risk: if Telemetry interface is not consistently implemented across modules, cycle time monitoring will be incomplete.
Network Link is a shared dependency for both Teleoperation (DP-2.B) and Telemetry (DP-2.D). If Network Link performance degrades, both sub-DPs are affected, creating a coupling risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of the system-level cycle time KPI, but the DP is inherently diffused across sub-modules. Recommend decomposing FR-2 into sub-FRs and assigning each sub-DP to a specific module (Vehicle for DP-2.A, Teleoperation for DP-2.B, Safety Services for DP-2.C, NGHE Solution for DP-2.D). This will reduce diffusion and enable clearer ownership boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is feasible if Telemetry interface is properly implemented across all modules. However, root-cause analysis of cycle time failures requires detailed telemetry (sub-cycle timestamps, latency events, safety interventions) that may not be available if Telemetry interface is not sufficiently granular. Recommend defining Telemetry schema and sampling requirements before proceeding to implementation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of cycle time responsibility is unclear at this level. NGHE Solution owns the KPI, but the actual performance is determined by Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Recommend establishing explicit cycle time budgets and performance contracts for each sub-module. Each sub-module should be responsible for meeting its allocated budget; NGHE Solution is responsible for aggregating performance and reporting KPI.

recommended_revisions: Decompose FR-2 into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) aligned with sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution). This will enable independent DP selection and reduce coupling.

Define explicit cycle time budgets for each sub-system (e.g., Vehicle: 100s, Teleoperation latency overhead: 10s, Safety overhead: 5s, total: 115s). Ensure budgets are achievable and compatible with CT_target.

Specify Telemetry interface schema and sampling requirements to support cycle time monitoring and root-cause analysis. Include sub-cycle timestamps, latency events, safety intervention events, and performance metrics from each sub-module.

Establish performance contracts between NGHE Solution and sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Each sub-module should commit to meeting its allocated cycle time budget and reporting performance metrics via Telemetry interface.

Conduct early coordination between Safety Manager and Project Manager to ensure safety constraints are compatible with cycle time target. If safety overhead exceeds safety_overhead_budget, revise CT_target or safety constraints.

decision.justification: FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is a coupled, system-level requirement that cannot be satisfied by a single DP or assigned to a single module. The selected DP (integrated cycle time optimization) is diffused across four sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution) and does not satisfy the independence axiom. Decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) is necessary to decouple the requirement and enable independent DP selection and module assignment. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify ownership boundaries and reduce integration risk.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets (load_budget, travel_budget, dump_budget).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification and root-cause analysis.

revise.why: FR-2 is too broad and coupled to be addressed at a single decomposition level. Decomposing into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) will enable independent DP selection for each sub-system and reduce integration complexity. Each sub-FR will be assigned to a specific module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution) with clear ownership and performance contracts.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within the physical constraints of the vehicle and network infrastructure.
Duty cycle is well-defined and stable (load mass, travel distance, terrain, ambient conditions).
Network latency is ≤ latency_budget (assumed 200ms); if actual latency is higher, cycle time target may be unachievable.
Safety overhead is < safety_overhead_budget (assumed 5% of total cycle time); if actual overhead is higher, cycle time target may be unachievable.
Operator skill and tele-operator latency tolerance are consistent across test and deployment.
Telemetry interface can be implemented with sufficient granularity to support root-cause analysis.
open_risks: Risk: Duty cycle definition is ambiguous. Recommend establishing a detailed duty cycle specification (load mass, travel distance, terrain profile, ambient conditions, operator skill level) before proceeding to implementation.

Risk: Safety constraints may be incompatible with cycle time target. Recommend early coordination between Safety Manager and Project Manager to establish safety_overhead_budget and ensure feasibility.
Risk: Network Link performance (latency, jitter, bandwidth) is a critical dependency. Recommend establishing explicit SLA for Network Link and contingency plans if SLA is not met.
Risk: Telemetry interface may not be sufficiently granular to support root-cause analysis. Recommend defining Telemetry schema and sampling requirements before proceeding to implementation.
Risk: Operator skill and tele-operator latency tolerance may vary. Recommend establishing operator training requirements and latency tolerance thresholds.
evidence_needed: Detailed duty cycle specification (load mass, travel distance, terrain profile, ambient conditions, operator skill level).
CT_target value and acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle).
Network Link SLA (latency, jitter, bandwidth) and contingency plans.
Telemetry interface schema and sampling requirements.
Operator training requirements and latency tolerance thresholds.
Vehicle actuation performance data (load cycle time, travel speed, dump cycle time) under agreed duty cycle.
Teleoperation latency measurements under agreed network conditions.
Safety system activation frequency and overhead under agreed duty cycle.
